records.ety: [G. furnieren, fourniren, fr. F. fournir to furnish. See Furnish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Veneered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Veneering.]

defs: To overlay or plate with a thin layer of wood or other material for outer finish or decoration; as, to veneer a piece of furniture with mahogany. Used also figuratively.
quotes: As a rogue in grain Veneered with sanctimonious theory.

records.ety: [Cf. G. furnier or fournier. See Veneer, v. t.]

defs: A thin leaf or layer of a more valuable or beautiful material for overlaying an inferior one, especially such a thin leaf of wood to be glued to a cheaper wood; hence, external show; gloss; false pretense.
